evaluation.name: CausalVQA
evaluation.scale: 1,586 items; 5 question types; 273-participant human baseline
evaluation.dimensions: counterfactual reasoning; hypothetical outcomes; anticipation; planning; description; accuracy by difficulty; temporal-evidence dependence

evaluation.name: IntPhys 2
evaluation.scale: 1,416 videos; 4 physical conditions; 3 splits
evaluation.dimensions: object permanence; object immutability; spatiotemporal continuity; solidity; camera motion; difficulty; classification accuracy

evaluation.name: MVPBench
evaluation.scale: 1,211 examples; 4 major categories
evaluation.dimensions: answer correctness; step accuracy; graph consistency; reasoning quality; path diversity; reasoning efficiency; visual grounding

evaluation.name: MiraBench
evaluation.scale: 906 videos; 16,704 annotation decisions; 12 model configurations
evaluation.dimensions: physics adherence; action-following fidelity; failure preservation; optimism bias; object-state consistency; kinematic compliance; evaluator agreement

evaluation.name: Beyond Simulation
evaluation.scale: 4,393 scenarios; 32 predicted futures per scenario
evaluation.dimensions: trajectory accuracy; causal-agent response; ego-action conditioning; uncontrollable-object sensitivity; reactivity; map and kinematic compliance; interaction realism
evaluation.dateBasis: Formal venue or official public release: 2025-05

evaluation.name: WorldMark
evaluation.scale: 500 cases; 100 images; 15 action sequences; 6 models
evaluation.dimensions: visual and imaging quality; translation and rotation alignment; 3D reprojection; state consistency; content consistency; style consistency

evaluation.name: StEvo-Bench
evaluation.scale: 225 tasks; 6 evolution categories; 11 model families
evaluation.dimensions: state progress; physical plausibility; temporal and scene coherence; occlusion control; illumination control; camera-lookaway compliance

evaluation.name: OopsieVerse
evaluation.scale: 32 household tasks; 2 simulator backends; 3 damage modalities
evaluation.dimensions: task completion; safe task completion; object health; mechanical damage; thermal damage; fluid damage; sim-to-real safety

evaluation.name: LogicGaze
evaluation.scale: 40K video segments; 5K images
evaluation.dimensions: causal validation; grounded narrative synthesis; perturbation rejection; causal-step consistency; hallucination resistance; accuracy and F1

evaluation.name: Morpheus
evaluation.scale: 130 real-world videos; 16 controlled experiment variants
evaluation.dimensions: energy conservation; momentum consistency; acceleration and force laws; ODE compliance; trajectory validity; depth consistency; physics-informed score

evaluation.name: ImageTime
evaluation.scale: 750 cases; 375 action concepts; 22 domains; 8 models
evaluation.dimensions: entity persistence; object-state consistency; spatial layout; temporal ordering; causal-process consistency; interaction fidelity; counterfactual constraints; visual quality

evaluation.name: RoboVerse
evaluation.scale: 1,000+ tasks; 10M+ transitions; multiple simulators and embodiments
evaluation.dimensions: task success; imitation learning; reinforcement learning; cross-simulator transfer; cross-embodiment generalization; policy execution; standardized logging

evaluation.name: MORSE-500
evaluation.scale: 500 scripted videos; 6 reasoning categories
evaluation.dimensions: temporal reasoning; physical reasoning; spatial reasoning; planning; mathematical reasoning; abstract reasoning; difficulty and distractor robustness

evaluation.name: Envision
evaluation.scale: 1,000 four-stage sequences; 6 disciplines; 15 models
evaluation.dimensions: spatiotemporal consistency; physical reliability; dynamics and interactivity; causal order; basic properties; aesthetics; robustness and stability

evaluation.name: T2VPhysBench
evaluation.scale: 84 prompts; 12 physical laws; 10 video models
evaluation.dimensions: Newtonian motion; gravitation; energy, mass, linear-momentum and angular-momentum conservation; Hooke, Snell, reflection and Bernoulli laws; hint sensitivity; counterfactual compliance

evaluation.name: Echo-Memory
evaluation.scale: 4 memory families; 3 evaluation branches; 160 open-domain return sources
evaluation.dimensions: camera-action replay; in-domain loop closure; open-domain semantic return; identity preservation; context capacity; memory compression; read-out access; recurrence; PSNR, SSIM and LPIPS

evaluation.name: Software World Models
evaluation.scale: 435 examples; 12 repositories; 12 models
evaluation.dimensions: test outcome; exception class; wall-clock time; peak memory; method and line profiler ranking; calibration; execution scope; NDCG and recall

evaluation.name: VideoScience-Bench
evaluation.scale: 200 prompts; 14 topics; 103 concepts; 7 models
evaluation.dimensions: prompt consistency; phenomenon congruency; correct dynamics; immutability; spatiotemporal coherence; physics and chemistry concept grounding; human-judge alignment

evaluation.name: MSVBench
evaluation.scale: 20 stories; 20 video-generation systems
evaluation.dimensions: visual quality; story-video alignment; narrative coherence; face, character and background consistency; style continuity; physical interaction; human rank alignment

evaluation.name: MuSS
evaluation.scale: 30K+ multi-shot clips; 1,000+ hours; 3,000+ films
evaluation.dimensions: narrative effectiveness; cross-shot visual stability; subject consistency and recall; viewpoint and pose diversity; visual logic; anti-copy-paste behavior; camera dynamics

evaluation.name: PhyCoBench
evaluation.scale: 120 prompts; 7 physical categories; 4 video models
evaluation.dimensions: gravity; collision; vibration; friction; fluid dynamics; projectile motion; rotation; optical-flow prediction; future-frame consistency; human ranking agreement

evaluation.name: VideoPhy-2
evaluation.scale: 197 actions; 3,940 prompts; 6,800 videos; 102K annotations
evaluation.dimensions: prompt adherence; action completion; physical likelihood; rule following and violation; contact and interaction; conservation laws; human and automatic evaluation

evaluation.name: ENACT
evaluation.scale: 8,972 QA pairs; 2 world-modeling tasks; 3–10-step horizons
evaluation.dimensions: forward state prediction; inverse action inference; temporal ordering; long-horizon memory; contact and state-change reasoning; viewpoint robustness; task and sequence accuracy

evaluation.name: What-If World
evaluation.scale: 319 intervention pairs; 6 variable primitives; 9 models
evaluation.dimensions: input adherence; physical plausibility; environment stability; outcome separation; intervention sensitivity; paired-versus-single consistency; driving and manipulation effects

evaluation.name: Relative Rollout Assessment
evaluation.scale: 150 rollouts; 5 tasks; 30 videos per task
evaluation.dimensions: 3D geometric consistency; optical-flow and photometric consistency; relative anomaly score; physical integrity; rollout filtering; policy-success alignment

evaluation.name: WorldRoamBench
evaluation.scale: 600+ cases; 10+ models; 10–60 s WASD interactions
evaluation.dimensions: per-frame and trajectory action following; segment-level visual drift; mechanics; optics; 3D consistency; scene memory; subject memory; first- and third-person stability

evaluation.name: PhysicsMind
evaluation.scale: 81 videos; 122 VQA pairs; 81 generation cases; 3 mechanics domains
evaluation.dimensions: center of mass; lever equilibrium; inertia; final-state accuracy; segmentation IoU and centroid error; trajectory RMSE; directional, speed and acceleration consistency; sim-to-real transfer

evaluation.name: ScratchWorld
evaluation.scale: 659 examples; 4 tasks; 4 evidence modalities
evaluation.dimensions: next-state prediction; changed fields and values; hidden-state tracking; causal rule-chain attribution; counterfactual outcomes; intervention sensitivity; copy resistance; perturbation robustness

evaluation.name: BilliardPhys-Bench
evaluation.scale: 200 scenes at each of 5 horizons; 3 tasks; 7 balls per scene
evaluation.dimensions: ball-to-ball collision; wall collision; final 2D coordinates; friction and elastic dynamics; multi-object tracking; event accuracy; coordinate error; 1–5 s temporal scaling

evaluation.name: RoboWorld
evaluation.scale: 9 policy variants; 3 views; 300-frame / 20 s closed-loop rollouts
evaluation.dimensions: closed-loop task success; policy ranking correlation; long-horizon rollout quality; task progress; action-state consistency; inference throughput; judge robustness

evaluation.name: WoW-World-Eval
evaluation.scale: 609 manipulation samples; 22 metrics; 5 capability groups
evaluation.dimensions: perception; planning; future prediction; physical law; execution accuracy; instruction alignment; generalization; human preference; inverse-dynamics recoverability

evaluation.name: ActVideoGen-Bench
evaluation.scale: 300 prompts; 3 difficulty levels; 1 to 5+ action steps
evaluation.dimensions: action completeness; action smoothness; object interaction; physical fidelity; visual quality; temporal coherence; subject and background consistency; procedural difficulty

evaluation.name: PQSG / FinePhyEval
evaluation.scale: 65 prompts; 3 generation models; graph-structured human annotations
evaluation.dimensions: object existence and state; action execution; physical-law adherence; dependency validity; localized failure attribution; human correlation; question generation and answering reliability

evaluation.name: SafeVLA-Bench
evaluation.scale: 2 host benchmarks; 6 VLA models; 8 safety-specification families
evaluation.dimensions: native task success; safety satisfaction; successful-but-unsafe rate; violation severity; contact force; bystander displacement; object stability; joint torque; self-collision

evaluation.name: SIMMER
evaluation.scale: 100 cooking scripts; 77 actions; 262 objects; ~46,800 interactions
evaluation.dimensions: precondition satisfaction; explicit and implicit state tracking; immediate failure; latent hazard; irreversible consequence; plan completion; counterfactual foresight; long-horizon action sequencing

evaluation.name: ContactWorld
evaluation.scale: 12 contact-rich manipulation tasks; visual, geometric and tactile-force modalities
evaluation.task: Multimodal contact-state prediction and model-based manipulation planning
evaluation.dimensions: future object and contact-state prediction; action conditioning; tactile-force representation; contact dynamics; task success; multimodal fusion; planning under physical interaction

evaluation.name: RoboTwin-MeM
evaluation.scale: 8 non-Markovian task families; 17 simulation and 4 real-world tasks; ~430–1,544 average steps
evaluation.task: Long-horizon embodied memory and action evaluation
evaluation.dimensions: past-event retrieval; persistent object and scene state; keyframe memory; long-context action selection; temporal credit assignment; simulation success; real-world task completion

evaluation.name: VideoPhy
evaluation.scale: Human-labeled generated videos across diverse physical interactions
evaluation.dimensions: object dynamics; interaction outcomes; physical plausibility; prompt adherence; automated-human agreement

evaluation.name: VisPhyBench
evaluation.scale: Multiple physics scenes; 5 model backbones; 2 executable rendering engines
evaluation.task: Code-driven physical scene reconstruction and rollout
evaluation.dimensions: scene reconstruction; physical parameterization; temporal behavior; semantic consistency; text-video agreement; executable-law grounding

evaluation.name: Physion++
evaluation.scale: Approximately 2,000 dynamic physical scenarios
evaluation.dimensions: mass; friction; elasticity; object interaction; online inference; long-term dependency; human alignment

evaluation.name: Meta-World
evaluation.scale: 50 manipulation tasks; ML1, ML10 and ML45 protocols
evaluation.task: Multi-task skill learning and held-out task adaptation
evaluation.dimensions: task success; state-conditioned control; action execution; multi-task transfer; few-shot adaptation; task generalization

evaluation.name: LIBERO
evaluation.scale: 130 standardized tasks across 4 benchmark suites
evaluation.task: Sequential manipulation learning and knowledge transfer
evaluation.dimensions: task success; spatial and object knowledge; goal transfer; long-horizon procedure; forward transfer; backward transfer; task-order robustness

evaluation.name: robomimic
evaluation.scale: Multiple simulated and real-robot tasks with machine and human datasets
evaluation.dimensions: trajectory history; visual and low-dimensional state; action prediction; task success; dataset quality; temporal abstraction; algorithm reproducibility

evaluation.name: robosuite
evaluation.scale: Modular robots, grippers, controllers and standardized manipulation tasks
evaluation.task: Physics-based manipulation learning and control evaluation
evaluation.dimensions: contact dynamics; robot and object state; controller action; task success; sensor observations; reproducibility; sim-to-real experimentation

evaluation.name: ManiSkill3
evaluation.scale: GPU-parallel environments with diverse robots, scenes and tasks
evaluation.task: Scalable manipulation training, generation and evaluation
evaluation.dimensions: physical dynamics; visual and geometric state; action execution; task success; rendering throughput; parallel simulation; generalization

evaluation.name: SIMPLER
evaluation.scale: 2 common robot setups with matched simulated manipulation tasks
evaluation.dimensions: task success; real-sim performance correlation; policy ranking; visual matching; dynamics calibration; mean maximum rank violation

evaluation.name: BEHAVIOR-1K
evaluation.scale: 1,000 everyday activities with realistic scenes, objects and state transitions
evaluation.dimensions: object and relation state; physical interaction; temporal procedure; navigation; manipulation; task completion; generalization
evaluation.dateBasis: Formal venue or official public release: 2023-11

evaluation.name: VLABench
evaluation.scale: Large compositional manipulation suite with task, object and scene variations
evaluation.dimensions: instruction grounding; object and spatial state; action sequence; task progress; success; compositional and out-of-distribution generalization

evaluation.name: RoboTwin 2.0
evaluation.scale: Dozens of bimanual tasks with large-scale randomized demonstrations
evaluation.task: Robust bimanual manipulation and sim-to-real evaluation
evaluation.dimensions: task success; object and scene state; coordinated action; instruction variation; domain randomization; robustness; sim-to-real transfer

evaluation.name: CARLA
evaluation.scale: Configurable towns, weather, traffic, sensors and driving routes
evaluation.dimensions: vehicle and traffic dynamics; route progress; collision; lane state; control; weather and town generalization; sensor perception

evaluation.name: nuPlan
evaluation.scale: Approximately 1,500 driving hours across 4 cities

evaluation.name: Occ3D

evaluation.dimensions: voxel geometry; semantic class; visibility; occlusion; free and occupied space; class IoU; scene completion

evaluation.name: NAVSIM

evaluation.dimensions: future trajectory; collision; drivable area; time-to-collision; comfort; route progress; ego dynamics; planning score

evaluation.name: Habitat
evaluation.scale: Thousands of indoor 3D spaces across multiple scene datasets

evaluation.dimensions: 3D scene and sensor state; action execution; geodesic distance; success; path length; SPL; transfer across environments

evaluation.name: AI2-THOR
evaluation.scale: 120 interactive kitchen, living-room, bedroom and bathroom scenes
evaluation.task: Visual navigation and household object interaction
evaluation.dimensions: object state and affordance; spatial relation; navigation; manipulation action; task success; scene and object generalization

evaluation.name: ALFRED
evaluation.scale: Approximately 8,000 demonstrations and 25,000 language instructions
evaluation.dimensions: instruction grounding; task progress; object state; navigation; manipulation sequence; goal-condition success; unseen-scene generalization

evaluation.name: TextWorld
evaluation.scale: Procedurally generated games with controllable maps, objects, quests and action depth
evaluation.dimensions: hidden state tracking; entity and relation state; action preconditions and effects; exploration; plan length; score; quest completion

evaluation.name: ScienceWorld
evaluation.scale: 30 task families with thousands of procedurally varied instances
evaluation.task: Interactive scientific experimentation and goal completion
evaluation.dimensions: object and process state; temporal change; action sequence; scientific reasoning; subgoal progress; task score; generalization

evaluation.name: WebArena
evaluation.scale: 812 tasks across 5 self-hosted realistic websites
evaluation.task: Long-horizon browser interaction and transaction completion
evaluation.dimensions: website and account state; navigation; form and tool action; cross-site dependency; task progress; functional correctness; success

evaluation.name: VisualWebArena
evaluation.scale: 910 visually grounded tasks across 3 realistic websites
evaluation.dimensions: visual and DOM state; image grounding; navigation and form action; persistent website change; task progress; functional correctness; success

evaluation.name: AppWorld
evaluation.scale: 9 apps, 457 APIs and 750 tasks with reproducible world states
evaluation.dimensions: database and user state; API preconditions and effects; transaction sequence; cross-app dependency; functional correctness; task success

evaluation.name: AndroidWorld
evaluation.scale: 116 task templates across 20 Android apps with dynamic instantiation
evaluation.dimensions: device and app state; visual grounding; tap, scroll and text action; task progress; dynamic variation; programmatic success

evaluation.name: ToolSandbox
evaluation.scale: Hundreds of scenarios spanning state dependency, canonicalization, insufficient information and distraction
evaluation.dimensions: hidden service state; dialogue context; tool preconditions and effects; milestone order; state similarity; task completion

evaluation.name: Tau-bench
evaluation.scale: Airline and retail domains with tools, policies, users and hundreds of tasks
evaluation.dimensions: database state; user goal; dialogue state; tool sequence; policy compliance; final-state correctness; pass^k reliability

evaluation.name: Tau2-bench
evaluation.scale: Multiple service domains with simulated users, tools, policies and stateful tasks
evaluation.task: Cooperative user-agent control of shared task state
evaluation.dimensions: shared and private state; user action; agent tool action; information exchange; policy compliance; final-state correctness; reliability

evaluation.name: CALVIN
evaluation.scale: 24 hours of teleoperated play; 34 tasks; five-task evaluation sequences
evaluation.dimensions: persistent object state; task sequence; instruction grounding; action execution; atomic success; completed sequence length; environment generalization

evaluation.name: SafeBench
evaluation.scale: 2,352 safety-critical scenarios across 8 scenario types
evaluation.dimensions: collision; route completion; time-to-collision; lane invasion; red-light violation; planning and control; scenario difficulty

evaluation.name: Bench2Drive
evaluation.scale: 44 scenario types with 2 million annotated frames
evaluation.dimensions: perception; dynamic interaction; route planning; vehicle control; scenario success; infractions; driving score; ability-wise performance

evaluation.name: Waymax
evaluation.scale: Large-scale Waymo motion scenarios with GPU/TPU-parallel simulation
evaluation.dimensions: road and agent state; multi-agent dynamics; vehicle action; collision; off-road behavior; imitation; route progress; simulation throughput

evaluation.name: MetaDrive
evaluation.scale: Procedurally generated unlimited road and traffic scenarios
evaluation.task: Closed-loop driving policy training and generalization
evaluation.dimensions: map and traffic state; vehicle dynamics; control; collision; route completion; safety; distribution shift; scenario generalization

evaluation.name: AdvSim
evaluation.scale: Adversarial variants of real-world traffic scenarios
evaluation.dimensions: multi-agent trajectory; physical and map feasibility; planner collision; safety margin; route behavior; scenario realism; adversarial difficulty

evaluation.name: SAFE-SIM
evaluation.scale: Large sets of controllable safety-critical traffic rollouts
evaluation.dimensions: future traffic state; actor interaction; collision risk; controllability; realism; diversity; planner response; safety outcome

evaluation.name: Safety2Drive
evaluation.scale: Large scenario suite spanning diverse safety-critical interactions
evaluation.dimensions: collision; traffic-rule violation; route completion; interactive response; scenario success; difficulty; robustness; closed-loop score

evaluation.name: Bench2Drive-Robust
evaluation.scale: Multiple deployment perturbation families and severities over Bench2Drive scenarios
evaluation.dimensions: sensor state; calibration; appearance and weather shift; vehicle dynamics; route completion; collision; infraction; degradation across severity

evaluation.name: Procgen
evaluation.scale: 16 games with procedurally unlimited levels
evaluation.task: Visual game control and out-of-distribution level generalization
evaluation.dimensions: dynamic game state; discrete action; episodic return; sample efficiency; train-test level gap; procedural generalization

evaluation.name: MineRL
evaluation.scale: Over 60 million state-action pairs from human gameplay
evaluation.dimensions: visual and inventory state; action sequence; resource acquisition; crafting progress; reward; task success; data efficiency

evaluation.name: MineDojo
evaluation.scale: 3,142 tasks with wiki, video and forum knowledge resources
evaluation.dimensions: world and inventory state; navigation; manipulation; crafting; long-horizon progress; task success; knowledge transfer

evaluation.name: ViZDoom
evaluation.scale: Configurable Doom scenarios with high-throughput simulation
evaluation.dimensions: visual and game state; action execution; navigation; resource and health state; reward; scenario completion; simulation throughput

evaluation.name: Craftax
evaluation.scale: Procedurally unlimited worlds with 65 achievements
evaluation.dimensions: world and inventory state; temporal survival; navigation; resource interaction; crafting sequence; combat; achievements; return

evaluation.name: WorkArena
evaluation.scale: 29 task types with randomized instances in ServiceNow
evaluation.dimensions: application and record state; navigation; form action; workflow sequence; task correctness; success; instance variation

evaluation.name: BrowserGym
evaluation.scale: Unified access to multiple web benchmark families and thousands of tasks
evaluation.dimensions: browser state; multimodal observation; action execution; task reset; reproducibility; validator outcome; cross-environment performance

evaluation.name: OSWorld
evaluation.scale: 369 tasks across Ubuntu, Windows and macOS applications
evaluation.dimensions: operating-system and application state; visual grounding; mouse and keyboard action; cross-app workflow; functional correctness; task success

evaluation.name: VLN-CE
evaluation.scale: Thousands of instruction-path episodes in continuous Matterport3D environments
evaluation.dimensions: agent pose and scene state; instruction grounding; low-level navigation action; goal distance; success; SPL; seen and unseen environment transfer

evaluation.name: ALFWorld
evaluation.scale: Thousands of aligned textual and embodied household tasks
evaluation.task: Long-horizon instruction execution and cross-modal transfer
evaluation.dimensions: object and relation state; temporal task progress; navigation; manipulation action; text-visual transfer; task success

evaluation.name: VirtualHome
evaluation.scale: 2,821 activities represented by executable programs in multiple 3D homes
evaluation.dimensions: entity and relation state; action preconditions and effects; temporal sequence; program executability; activity completion; scene grounding

evaluation.name: BEHAVIOR
evaluation.scale: 100 household activities with realistic scenes and object interactions
evaluation.dimensions: object and relation state; physical interaction; temporal sequence; navigation; manipulation; goal-condition success; task completion

evaluation.name: iGibson
evaluation.scale: 15 fully interactive indoor scenes with robots and articulated objects
evaluation.dimensions: 3D scene and object state; physical dynamics; sensor observation; navigation and manipulation action; collision; task success; sim-to-real transfer

evaluation.name: PhysiCa-Bench
evaluation.scale: 10,000 manually validated direct and counterfactual physical-reasoning examples
evaluation.task: Predict physical outcomes before and after controlled interventions
evaluation.dimensions: initial and final physical state; object properties; temporal outcome prediction; causal intervention effect; counterfactual consistency; direct-question accuracy

evaluation.name: WoW-bench
evaluation.target: Enterprise Tool-Using Agent and World Model
evaluation.scale: 234 tasks in a ServiceNow environment with 4,000+ business rules and 55 active workflows
evaluation.task: Predict hidden workflow effects and complete constrained enterprise actions
evaluation.dimensions: database state transition; cascading side effects; partial observability; workflow prediction; constraint reliability; tool action; task completion; oracle-audit gap

evaluation.name: VideoScore-Bench-v2
evaluation.target: Generated-Video Evaluator and Video World Model
evaluation.scale: 27,168 human-annotated videos with a 500-video in-domain benchmark split
evaluation.task: Score and explain generated-video quality, text alignment, and physical consistency
evaluation.dimensions: visual quality; text-video alignment; temporal coherence; physical and commonsense consistency; rationale quality; pairwise preference; point-score correlation

evaluation.name: PhysicsArena
evaluation.scale: 5,103 multimodal physics problems across three difficulty levels and fourteen topic groups
evaluation.task: Identify physical variables, formulate processes, and derive solutions from text and diagrams
evaluation.dimensions: entity and field state; geometry and structure; interaction; external influence; process relationship; physical-law application; solution accuracy; difficulty transfer

evaluation.name: CaST-Bench
evaluation.scale: 2,066 questions over 1,015 videos with grounded causal-chain annotations
evaluation.task: Answer causal video questions while localizing a multi-step evidence chain
evaluation.dimensions: event order; cause and effect; predictive anticipation; counterfactual reasoning; temporal localization; spatial grounding; answer accuracy; evidence-answer faithfulness

evaluation.name: PASTURE
evaluation.scale: PASTURE task suite evaluated with over 90,000 aggregate navigation episodes across three environments
evaluation.task: Find unseen objects from free-form descriptions without in-domain navigation training
evaluation.dimensions: object identity and attributes; spatial relation; hidden-object relation; distractor discrimination; exploration; localization; navigation action; success; SPL

evaluation.name: OccSTeP
evaluation.scale: Occ3D-nuScenes sequences transformed into four temporal-persistence challenge scenarios
evaluation.task: Forecast future occupancy reactively and under queried ego trajectories despite corrupted history
evaluation.dimensions: semantic occupancy; geometric persistence; long-horizon memory; missing or incorrect observations; ego-motion prediction; action-conditioned forecast; IoU; mIoU; planning response

evaluation.name: DisCoBench
evaluation.scale: 300 compositional image-action cases, 200 dynamic exploration videos, and long-horizon sequences of at least 241 frames
evaluation.task: Generate short, dynamic, and long-horizon world rollouts that follow discrete camera-action sequences
evaluation.dimensions: action adherence; compositional control; temporal coherence; subject consistency; background consistency; motion smoothness; dynamic-scene fidelity; long-horizon stability; visual quality

evaluation.name: WorldTasks-Bench
evaluation.scale: 146,440 training task prompts derived from 20,000 images and a 200-pair held-out benchmark
evaluation.task: Generate a video in which the requested agent completes an embodied task with coherent world dynamics
evaluation.dimensions: task completion; agent identity and engagement; navigation; spatial positioning; object interaction; scene perception; multi-step control; physical plausibility; temporal coherence

evaluation.name: Latent Video World-Model Robustness Protocol
evaluation.scale: Five controlled evaluation axes on Something-Something v2, including a 600-video discriminability slice and multi-severity perturbations
evaluation.task: Test whether frozen video representations preserve usable action and dynamics information under corruption, occlusion, fine-grained contact changes, and time reversal
evaluation.dimensions: feature discriminability; corruption robustness; contact-state sensitivity; missing-observation robustness; temporal direction; top-1 accuracy; accuracy retention; representation similarity; semantic reversal coherence

evaluation.name: LiveBench
evaluation.scale: 100 scenes and 400 camera-controlled evaluation sequences; each sequence spans 4 rounds and 260 frames at 16 FPS
evaluation.task: Move the camera away from an active event and revisit while preserving background, entity identity, elapsed dynamics, and scripted outcomes
evaluation.dimensions: out-of-sight event evolution; event success and presence; entity identity; spatial memory; background consistency; 3D dynamic-state agreement; camera revisit control; long-horizon video quality

evaluation.name: LiDARCrafter Benchmark
evaluation.scale: nuScenes-based scene graphs with 10,000 generated samples for scene-level and layout-level evaluation
evaluation.task: Generate or edit temporally coherent LiDAR scenes that follow object layouts, spatial relations, motion states, trajectories, and text controls
evaluation.dimensions: range and point-cloud fidelity; BEV distribution; object detectability and geometry; temporal coherence; spatial-relation consistency; motion-state consistency; trajectory adherence; edit controllability

evaluation.name: Video World-Model Policy Evaluation
evaluation.target: Robot Policy and Action-Conditioned Video World Model
evaluation.scale: Synthetic RoboMimic and real-robot tasks with demonstration and policy-rollout datasets studied from 0.3M to 1.1M transitions
evaluation.task: Roll out a policy's action sequence in a video world model, judge task completion, estimate policy value, and recover the ranking measured by execution
evaluation.dimensions: policy-ranking agreement; predicted-versus-real value correlation; task-completion prediction; rollout fidelity; OOD action handling; physical consistency; calibration; dataset and pretraining sensitivity

evaluation.name: EZSbench
evaluation.scale: Dual-branch real and synthetic zero-shot scene pool across robot, scene, task, and perspective axes, plus 200 structured action-to-video instances
evaluation.task: Generate physically plausible manipulation videos for unseen robot-task-scene combinations and follow specified end-effector and gripper trajectories
evaluation.dimensions: zero-shot generalization; physical realism; penetration and gravity violations; contact dynamics; instruction binding; action alignment; trajectory consistency; pixel and structural fidelity; temporal coherence

evaluation.name: 4D Occupancy Forecasting Protocol
evaluation.scale: nuScenes, KITTI-Odometry, and Argoverse 2 protocols with matched 1-second and 3-second future horizons across different LiDAR sensor rigs
evaluation.task: Predict future world occupancy or render future LiDAR while separating scene dynamics from ego motion, sensor sampling, visibility, and occlusion
evaluation.dimensions: continuous occupancy accuracy; occupied/free/unknown classification; geometric L1 error; point-cloud distance; short- and long-horizon forecasting; cross-sensor generalization; visibility awareness; static and dynamic geometry
